description:
## Problem

`check_pr_status` is a synchronous polling tool. Ava has to repeatedly call it in a loop, burning turns and blocking conversation while waiting for CI to complete. This doesn't scale with multiple PRs in flight and makes Ava appear stuck.

## Solution

Build a background PR watcher that fires a push notification into the active Ava chat session when a PR's check state changes. Ava can then respond in context — immediately if she's waiting, or when she next processes messages if she's doing other work.

## Components

### 1. `PRWatcherService` (apps/server/src/services/)
- Registry of watched PRs: `Map<prNumber, { sessionId, watchedSince, lastStatus }>`
- Polling loop (configurable interval, default 30s) as primary mechanism
- GitHub `check_suite` / `check_run` webhook events as secondary trigger (faster when available)
- Emits internal `pr_watch_resolved` event when all checks pass OR any check fails
- Auto-removes watch after resolution or timeout (configurable, default 30min)

### 2. GitHub webhook handler
- Register handler for `check_run` completed events in existing GitHub webhook route
- On receipt: check if PR is in the watcher registry, trigger resolution if all checks pass

### 3. `watch_pr` MCP/chat tool
- Accepts: `prNumber`, `sessionId` (injected server-side from auth context)
- Returns immediately with `{ watching: true, prNumber }`
- On resolution: server pushes WebSocket event to the session: `{ type: 'pr_watch_resolved', prNumber, status: 'passed' | 'failed', checks: [...] }`

### 4. Ava chat surface wiring
- WebSocket client in UI receives `pr_watch_resolved` events
- Injects as a new message into the active chat session
- Ava processes it and responds: merge, escalate, or report as appropriate

## Acceptance Criteria
- `watch_pr(1820)` returns immediately
- When all checks on PR #1820 pass, Ava receives a push notification in the active session without any polling
- If a check fails, Ava is notified with which check failed
- Watch auto-expires after 30 minutes if not resolved
- Polling fallback works in environments without GitHub webhook inbound access
- Existing `check_pr_status` tool remains available for one-off manual checks
title: Background PR watcher with Ava chat push notification

descriptionHistory.description:
## Problem

`check_pr_status` is a synchronous polling tool. Ava has to repeatedly call it in a loop, burning turns and blocking conversation while waiting for CI to complete. This doesn't scale with multiple PRs in flight and makes Ava appear stuck.

## Solution

Build a background PR watcher that fires a push notification into the active Ava chat session when a PR's check state changes. Ava can then respond in context — immediately if she's waiting, or when she next processes messages if she's doing other work.

## Components

### 1. `PRWatcherService` (apps/server/src/services/)
- Registry of watched PRs: `Map<prNumber, { sessionId, watchedSince, lastStatus }>`
- Polling loop (configurable interval, default 30s) as primary mechanism
- GitHub `check_suite` / `check_run` webhook events as secondary trigger (faster when available)
- Emits internal `pr_watch_resolved` event when all checks pass OR any check fails
- Auto-removes watch after resolution or timeout (configurable, default 30min)

### 2. GitHub webhook handler
- Register handler for `check_run` completed events in existing GitHub webhook route
- On receipt: check if PR is in the watcher registry, trigger resolution if all checks pass

### 3. `watch_pr` MCP/chat tool
- Accepts: `prNumber`, `sessionId` (injected server-side from auth context)
- Returns immediately with `{ watching: true, prNumber }`
- On resolution: server pushes WebSocket event to the session: `{ type: 'pr_watch_resolved', prNumber, status: 'passed' | 'failed', checks: [...] }`

### 4. Ava chat surface wiring
- WebSocket client in UI receives `pr_watch_resolved` events
- Injects as a new message into the active chat session
- Ava processes it and responds: merge, escalate, or report as appropriate

- `watch_pr(1820)` returns immediately
- When all checks on PR #1820 pass, Ava receives a push notification in the active session without any polling

- Watch auto-expires after 30 minutes if not resolved
- Polling fallback works in environments without GitHub webhook inbound access
